name: functions
context: Terraform built-in functions — string, numeric, collection, encoding, filesystem, date, hash, IP, and type conversion
info: Terraform provides a rich library of built-in functions callable from any HCL expression. Functions are organized into categories: string manipulation (format, join, split, replace, trim, regex, upper, lower, substr), numeric operations (min, max, ceil, floor, abs, parseint, pow), collection transforms (length, lookup, merge, keys, values, flatten, zipmap, concat, distinct, sort, contains, element, slice), encoding (jsonencode, jsondecode, yamlencode, yamldecode, base64encode, base64decode, csvdecode), filesystem (file, fileexists, templatefile, fileset, filebase64), date/time (timestamp, formatdate, timeadd, timecmp), hash/crypto (md5, sha1, sha256, sha512, bcrypt, uuid), IP networking (cidrsubnet, cidrhost, cidrnetmask), and type conversion (tostring, tonumber, tobool, tolist, toset, tomap, try, can). Functions are called with standard syntax: `function_name(arg1, arg2, ...)`. Terraform does not support user-defined functions. See [[terraform/hcl/expressions]] for how functions compose within expressions.
body:
# Terraform Built-in Functions

Terraform includes a comprehensive library of built-in functions that you call from any expression. The syntax is `function_name(arg1, arg2, ...)`. Terraform does not support user-defined functions — all available functions are built in.

Test functions interactively with `terraform console`:

```bash
$ terraform console
> upper("hello")
"HELLO"
> cidrsubnet("10.0.0.0/16", 8, 1)
"10.0.1.0/24"
> jsonencode({name = "web", port = 8080})
"{\"name\":\"web\",\"port\":8080}"
```

## String Functions

Manipulate and format strings:

```hcl
locals {
  # format — printf-style string formatting
  formatted = format("Hello, %s! You have %d items.", var.name, var.count)
  padded_id = format("%05d", var.id)  # "00042"

  # join / split
  csv_list   = join(",", ["a", "b", "c"])         # "a,b,c"
  parts      = split(",", "a,b,c")                 # ["a", "b", "c"]
  subnet_ids = join(",", aws_subnet.private[*].id)

  # replace
  sanitized = replace(var.name, "/[^a-zA-Z0-9-]/", "-")
  no_spaces = replace("hello world", " ", "_")

  # trim functions
  trimmed      = trim("  hello  ", " ")        # "hello"
  trim_prefix  = trimprefix("helloworld", "hello")  # "world"
  trim_suffix  = trimsuffix("hello.tf", ".tf")      # "hello"
  trimmed_s    = trimspace("  hello  \n")           # "hello"

  # Case conversion
  upper_name = upper("hello")      # "HELLO"
  lower_name = lower("HELLO")      # "hello"
  title_name = title("hello world") # "Hello World"

  # Substring and length
  sub  = substr("hello world", 0, 5)  # "hello"
  len  = strlen("hello")               # 5

  # Regex
  match   = regex("^([a-z]+)-([0-9]+)$", "web-42")         # ["web", "42"]
  matches = regexall("[a-z]+", "abc 123 def 456")            # ["abc", "def"]
  is_valid = can(regex("^t[23]\\.", var.instance_type))      # true/false

  # Other
  indented = indent(4, "line1\nline2\nline3")
  chomp_nl = chomp("hello\n")  # "hello"
}
```

## Numeric Functions

Perform mathematical operations:

```hcl
locals {
  min_val   = min(5, 12, 3)          # 3
  max_val   = max(5, 12, 3)          # 12
  ceiling   = ceil(4.3)              # 5
  floor_val = floor(4.7)             # 4
  absolute  = abs(-42)               # 42
  parsed    = parseint("FF", 16)     # 255
  power     = pow(2, 10)             # 1024
  sign_val  = signum(-5)             # -1
  log_val   = log(100, 10)           # 2

  # Common patterns
  az_index       = count.index % length(var.availability_zones)
  storage_gib    = ceil(var.storage_bytes / pow(1024, 3))
  half_instances = floor(var.instance_count / 2)
}
```

## Collection Functions

Transform lists, maps, and sets:

```hcl
locals {
  # length — works on lists, maps, and strings
  count = length(var.subnet_ids)       # 3

  # lookup — get a map value with a default
  ami = lookup(var.ami_map, var.region, "ami-default")

  # merge — combine maps (last wins on conflicts)
  all_tags = merge(local.common_tags, {
    Name = "${var.project}-web"
  })

  # keys / values — extract from a map
  tag_keys   = keys(local.common_tags)     # ["Environment", "ManagedBy", "Project"]
  tag_values = values(local.common_tags)   # ["prod", "terraform", "myapp"]

  # flatten — collapse nested lists
  all_subnets = flatten([
    module.vpc.public_subnets,
    module.vpc.private_subnets,
  ])

  # zipmap — create a map from parallel lists
  instance_map = zipmap(
    aws_instance.web[*].tags["Name"],
    aws_instance.web[*].id
  )

  # concat — join lists
  all_cidrs = concat(var.public_cidrs, var.private_cidrs)

  # distinct — remove duplicates
  unique_azs = distinct(var.availability_zones)

  # sort — alphabetical sort
  sorted_names = sort(var.names)

  # contains — check membership
  is_prod = contains(["prod", "production"], var.environment)

  # element — index into a list (wraps around)
  subnet = element(var.subnet_ids, count.index)

  # slice — extract a sub-list
  first_two = slice(var.list, 0, 2)

  # chunklist — split into fixed-size sub-lists
  batches = chunklist(var.instances, 3)

  # setproduct — cartesian product
  az_subnet_pairs = setproduct(var.azs, var.subnet_tiers)

  # setunion, setintersection, setsubtract
  all_groups    = setunion(var.admin_groups, var.dev_groups)
  common_groups = setintersection(var.admin_groups, var.dev_groups)
  only_admins   = setsubtract(var.admin_groups, var.dev_groups)

  # coalesce — first non-null, non-empty value
  name = coalesce(var.custom_name, "${var.project}-default")

  # coalescelist — first non-empty list
  subnets = coalescelist(var.custom_subnets, data.aws_subnets.default.ids)

  # one — extract single element or null
  instance = one(aws_instance.special[*].id)

  # compact — remove empty strings from a list
  clean_list = compact(["a", "", "b", "", "c"])  # ["a", "b", "c"]

  # range — generate sequence
  indices = range(0, 5)      # [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]
  evens   = range(0, 10, 2)  # [0, 2, 4, 6, 8]
}
```

## Encoding Functions

Serialize and deserialize structured data:

```hcl
locals {
  # JSON
  json_str  = jsonencode({
    name    = var.project
    port    = 8080
    enabled = true
    tags    = ["web", "api"]
  })
  json_obj  = jsondecode(file("${path.module}/config.json"))

  # YAML
  yaml_str  = yamlencode({
    apiVersion = "v1"
    kind       = "ConfigMap"
    metadata   = { name = var.name }
    data       = { key = "value" }
  })
  yaml_obj  = yamldecode(file("${path.module}/values.yaml"))

  # Base64
  encoded   = base64encode("Hello, World!")
  decoded   = base64decode("SGVsbG8sIFdvcmxkIQ==")

  # CSV
  csv_data  = csvdecode(file("${path.module}/users.csv"))
  # Returns list of maps: [{name = "alice", role = "admin"}, ...]

  # URL encoding
  url_safe  = urlencode("hello world&foo=bar")

  # Common patterns — IAM policy as JSON
  policy_json = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Effect   = "Allow"
      Action   = ["s3:GetObject", "s3:ListBucket"]
      Resource = [
        aws_s3_bucket.data.arn,
        "${aws_s3_bucket.data.arn}/*",
      ]
    }]
  })
}

resource "aws_iam_policy" "s3_read" {
  name   = "${var.project}-s3-read"
  policy = local.policy_json
}
```

## Filesystem Functions

Read files from disk at plan time:

```hcl
locals {
  # file — read a file as a string
  public_key = file("${path.module}/keys/id_rsa.pub")

  # fileexists — check if a file exists
  has_config = fileexists("${path.module}/custom.conf")

  # templatefile — render a template with variables
  user_data = templatefile("${path.module}/scripts/init.sh.tpl", {
    environment = var.environment
    db_host     = aws_db_instance.main.endpoint
    db_port     = 5432
    app_version = var.app_version
  })

  # fileset — glob for matching files
  config_files = fileset(path.module, "configs/*.json")

  # filebase64 — read file as base64
  lambda_zip = filebase64("${path.module}/dist/function.zip")

  # Common pattern: upload all config files
  configs = { for f in local.config_files : f => file("${path.module}/${f}") }
}

resource "aws_key_pair" "deployer" {
  key_name   = "${var.project}-deployer"
  public_key = local.public_key
}

resource "aws_instance" "web" {
  ami           = data.aws_ami.ubuntu.id
  instance_type = var.instance_type
  user_data     = local.user_data
}
```

Template files support `%{ if }` and `%{ for }` directives:

```bash
# init.sh.tpl
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

export ENVIRONMENT="${environment}"
export DB_HOST="${db_host}"
export DB_PORT="${db_port}"

%{ if environment == "prod" }
export LOG_LEVEL="warn"
%{ else }
export LOG_LEVEL="debug"
%{ endif }

%{ for pkg in packages }
apt-get install -y ${pkg}
%{ endfor }
```

## Date and Time Functions

Work with timestamps:

```hcl
locals {
  # timestamp — current UTC time in RFC3339
  now = timestamp()  # "2026-04-11T14:30:00Z"

  # formatdate — format a timestamp
  date_only = formatdate("YYYY-MM-DD", timestamp())        # "2026-04-11"
  readable  = formatdate("DD MMM YYYY hh:mm ZZZ", timestamp())  # "11 Apr 2026 14:30 UTC"
  file_safe = formatdate("YYYYMMDD-hhmmss", timestamp())   # "20260411-143000"

  # timeadd — add a duration to a timestamp
  expires_at  = timeadd(timestamp(), "720h")    # 30 days from now
  next_hour   = timeadd(timestamp(), "1h")
  ten_min_ago = timeadd(timestamp(), "-10m")

  # timecmp — compare timestamps: -1, 0, or 1
  is_expired = timecmp(var.cert_expiry, timestamp()) < 0
}

resource "aws_iam_access_key" "deploy" {
  user = aws_iam_user.deploy.name
}

resource "null_resource" "rotate_key" {
  triggers = {
    # Force rotation every 30 days
    rotation_date = formatdate("YYYY-MM", timestamp())
  }
}
```

## Hash and Crypto Functions

Generate hashes and unique identifiers:

```hcl
locals {
  # Hash functions
  md5_hash    = md5("hello")                  # "5d41402abc4b2a76b9719d911017c592"
  sha1_hash   = sha1("hello")                 # "aaf4c61ddcc5e8a2dabede0f3b482cd9aea9434d"
  sha256_hash = sha256("hello")               # "2cf24dba5fb0a30e..."
  sha512_hash = sha512("hello")               # "9b71d224bd62f378..."

  # bcrypt — password hashing
  hashed_pw = bcrypt(var.password)             # "$2a$10$..."
  custom_pw = bcrypt(var.password, 12)         # cost factor 12

  # uuid — generate a random UUID
  unique_id = uuid()                           # "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-..."

  # filemd5 / filesha256 / filesha512 — hash file contents
  lambda_hash = filesha256("${path.module}/dist/function.zip")

  # Common patterns
  config_hash   = sha256(jsonencode(var.config))  # Detect config changes
  resource_name = "${var.project}-${substr(md5(var.unique_input), 0, 8)}"
}

resource "aws_lambda_function" "api" {
  filename         = "${path.module}/dist/function.zip"
  source_code_hash = local.lambda_hash  # Triggers redeployment on code change
  function_name    = "${var.project}-api"
  handler          = "index.handler"
  runtime          = "nodejs20.x"
  role             = aws_iam_role.lambda.arn
}
```

## IP Network Functions

Calculate CIDR addresses and subnets:

```hcl
locals {
  # cidrsubnet — calculate a subnet CIDR within a network
  # cidrsubnet(prefix, newbits, netnum)
  public_subnet_1  = cidrsubnet("10.0.0.0/16", 8, 0)   # "10.0.0.0/24"
  public_subnet_2  = cidrsubnet("10.0.0.0/16", 8, 1)   # "10.0.1.0/24"
  private_subnet_1 = cidrsubnet("10.0.0.0/16", 8, 100) # "10.0.100.0/24"
  private_subnet_2 = cidrsubnet("10.0.0.0/16", 8, 101) # "10.0.101.0/24"

  # cidrhost — calculate a host IP within a network
  gateway_ip  = cidrhost("10.0.1.0/24", 1)    # "10.0.1.1"
  dns_server  = cidrhost("10.0.0.0/16", 2)    # "10.0.0.2" (AWS VPC DNS)

  # cidrnetmask — get the netmask for a CIDR
  netmask = cidrnetmask("10.0.0.0/16")         # "255.255.0.0"

  # cidrcontains — check if IP is in a CIDR (Terraform 1.8+)
  # is_internal = cidrcontains("10.0.0.0/8", var.ip_address)

  # Common pattern — generate subnets for all AZs
  public_subnets = [
    for i, az in var.availability_zones :
    cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 8, i)
  ]
  private_subnets = [
    for i, az in var.availability_zones :
    cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 8, i + 100)
  ]
}

resource "aws_subnet" "public" {
  count             = length(var.availability_zones)
  vpc_id            = aws_vpc.main.id
  cidr_block        = local.public_subnets[count.index]
  availability_zone = var.availability_zones[count.index]

  tags = merge(local.common_tags, {
    Name = "${var.project}-public-${count.index + 1}"
    Tier = "public"
  })
}
```

## Type Conversion Functions

Convert between types and handle errors:

```hcl
locals {
  # Explicit type conversion
  str_val  = tostring(42)              # "42"
  num_val  = tonumber("42")            # 42
  bool_val = tobool("true")            # true

  # Collection conversions
  list_val = tolist(["a", "b", "c"])   # list(string)
  set_val  = toset(["a", "b", "a"])    # set(string): {"a", "b"}
  map_val  = tomap({ a = 1, b = 2 })   # map(number)

  # try — return first expression that doesn't error
  port = try(var.config.port, var.default_port, 8080)
  db   = try(
    data.aws_db_instance.existing.endpoint,
    "localhost:5432"
  )

  # can — test if an expression succeeds (returns bool)
  has_port   = can(var.config.port)
  valid_cidr = can(cidrnetmask(var.cidr))
  valid_json = can(jsondecode(var.json_string))

  # Practical pattern — safe optional attribute access
  bucket_name = try(var.overrides.bucket_name, "${var.project}-default")

  # nonsensitive — remove sensitive marking (use carefully)
  debug_id = nonsensitive(var.secret_id)  # Only for debugging!

  # sensitive — mark a value as sensitive
  conn_string = sensitive("postgres://${var.user}:${var.pass}@${var.host}/db")

  # type — inspect type at console (debugging only)
  # > type(var.config)
  # object({ port = number, host = string })
}
```

`try` and `can` are especially useful in module development where inputs may be optional or structurally variable. Use `can()` in [[terraform/hcl/variables]] validation blocks to test complex conditions.

sections.title: String functions
sections.body: Key functions: `format(spec, vals...)` for printf-style formatting, `join(sep, list)` and `split(sep, string)` for joining/splitting, `replace(string, search, replace)` with regex support, `trim` / `trimprefix` / `trimsuffix` / `trimspace` for whitespace/prefix/suffix removal, `upper` / `lower` / `title` for case, `substr(string, offset, length)` for substrings, `strlen` for length, `regex(pattern, string)` / `regexall` for pattern matching, `indent(spaces, string)` for indentation, `chomp` to remove trailing newline.

sections.title: Numeric functions
sections.body: Key functions: `min(nums...)` / `max(nums...)` for min/max from a set, `ceil` / `floor` for rounding, `abs` for absolute value, `parseint(string, base)` for base conversion (e.g. hex), `pow(base, exp)` for exponentiation, `signum` for sign, `log(val, base)` for logarithm. Arithmetic operators (`+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, `%`) are part of expressions, not functions.

sections.title: Collection functions
sections.body: Key functions: `length` for size, `lookup(map, key, default)` for safe map access, `merge(maps...)` for combining maps, `keys` / `values` for extracting map parts, `flatten` for collapsing nested lists, `zipmap(keys, values)` for building maps from parallel lists, `concat(lists...)` for joining lists, `distinct` for deduplication, `sort` for alphabetical ordering, `contains(list, value)` for membership, `element(list, index)` for wrapped indexing, `slice(list, start, end)` for sub-lists, `compact` to remove empty strings, `coalesce` for first non-null, `range` for number sequences, `setunion` / `setintersection` / `setsubtract` for set operations, `chunklist` for batching, `one` for extracting single elements.

sections.title: Encoding functions
sections.body: Key functions: `jsonencode` / `jsondecode` for JSON serialization (essential for IAM policies, API configs), `yamlencode` / `yamldecode` for YAML (Kubernetes manifests, Helm values), `base64encode` / `base64decode` for Base64, `csvdecode` to parse CSV files into list-of-maps, `urlencode` for URL-safe strings. `jsonencode` is the most commonly used — produces compact JSON from HCL structures.

sections.title: Filesystem functions
sections.body: Key functions: `file(path)` reads a file as UTF-8 string, `fileexists(path)` checks existence, `templatefile(path, vars)` renders a template with variable substitution and `%{ if }` / `%{ for }` directives, `fileset(base, pattern)` globs for matching files, `filebase64(path)` reads as Base64. `filemd5` / `filesha256` / `filesha512` hash file contents (used for Lambda source_code_hash). All paths should use `path.module` or `path.root` for portability.

sections.title: Date and time functions
sections.body: Key functions: `timestamp()` returns current UTC time in RFC3339 format, `formatdate(spec, timestamp)` formats with specifiers (YYYY, MM, DD, hh, mm, ss, ZZZ), `timeadd(timestamp, duration)` adds a Go-style duration (e.g. "720h", "30m", "-1h"), `timecmp(a, b)` compares two timestamps returning -1, 0, or 1. Note: `timestamp()` changes every plan, so using it in resource attributes forces replacement — use `ignore_changes` or `triggers` to control this.

sections.title: Hash and crypto functions
sections.body: Key functions: `md5`, `sha1`, `sha256`, `sha512` produce hex-encoded hashes of strings, `filemd5` / `filesha256` / `filesha512` hash file contents, `bcrypt(string, cost)` produces bcrypt password hashes, `uuid()` generates a random v4 UUID. `sha256` + `jsonencode` is a common pattern for change detection triggers. `filesha256` is essential for Lambda `source_code_hash` to trigger redeployment on code changes.

sections.title: IP network functions
sections.body: Key functions: `cidrsubnet(prefix, newbits, netnum)` calculates a subnet within a CIDR (e.g. `cidrsubnet("10.0.0.0/16", 8, 1)` yields `"10.0.1.0/24"`), `cidrhost(prefix, hostnum)` computes a specific host IP, `cidrnetmask(prefix)` returns the dotted-decimal netmask. These are essential for VPC and subnet planning in AWS, GCP, and Azure. Combine with `for` expressions and `count` to generate subnets across availability zones programmatically.

sections.title: Type conversion functions
sections.body: Key functions: `tostring`, `tonumber`, `tobool` for primitive conversion, `tolist`, `toset`, `tomap` for collection conversion, `try(expr1, expr2, ...)` returns the first expression that doesn't error, `can(expr)` returns bool indicating whether an expression evaluates successfully. `try` and `can` are essential for defensive module code — safely accessing optional attributes, providing fallback values, and writing [[terraform/hcl/variables]] validation conditions. `sensitive` / `nonsensitive` control value redaction in output.

links.context: Parent HCL syntax and block structure
Variable validation blocks use can() and regex()

related.note: Functions are called within expressions to transform and compute values
Validation blocks use can() and regex() to enforce variable constraints
Functions operate on HCL value types defined by the language

frontmatter.author: dojo-community
frontmatter.audience: developers, AI agents, DevOps engineers, SREs

author: dojo-community
